hand_profile.script_style: Humanistic cursive with legal notarial features, typical of 17th-century Spanish documents. The hand is moderately sloped, with fluid, connected strokes and some flourished capitals.

letter_formations.u_v: Generally distinguishable; 'u' is rounded and open, while 'v' is pointed and often used at word-initial position.
letter_formations.f_s: Visually distinguishable; 'f' has a crossbar and descender, while 's' appears both as long-s and round-s.
letter_formations.s: Both long-s (ſ) in initial/medial positions and round-s in final positions are present.
letter_formations.r: Mostly straight r, with occasional use of a more rounded or rotunda form after certain letters.
letter_formations.a: Single-compartment, open at the top, sometimes with a slight flourish.
letter_formations.e: Small, open loop, sometimes with a slightly upward tail.

hand_profile.marginalia_position: upper left margin, faint and partially cut off
hand_profile.dropcap_description: Large, ornate capital 'E' at the beginning of the text, extending into the left margin.
special_challenges: Edge damage and loss of text on the right margin
Ink blots obscuring some words in the first line
Show-through from verso slightly interferes with legibility
curator_rules: u and v are used interchangeably
f and s are used interchangeably
ç old spelling is always modern z
accents are inconsistent — transcribe exactly as written
horizontal cap over letter means n follows
long-s and round-s both present — transcribe as written
abbreviation marks: transcribe the expansion not the symbol
